calendar.matters:
PETITION TO QUASH DEPOSITION SUBPOENA SERVED ON CHECKR, INC., OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, PETITION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 2029.600; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT
Off Calendar Apr-25-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PETITION TO QUASH DEPOSITION SUBPOENA SERVED ON CHECKR, INC., OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, PETITION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 2029.600.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Susan N. Nikdel, Esq., of TROUTMAN PEPPER HAMILTON SANDERS LLP, (949) 622-2447, for Petitioner.
No other appearances.

The matter is not argued/contested. The court adopts its tentative ruling. Petitioner Checkr, Inc. has filed a petition to quash a subpoena directed to the petitioner, a non-party in an out-of-state litigation pending in Pennsylvania. The deposition subpoena is directed to Checkr, Inc., and the deposition is to be conducted in San Francisco.
No opposition to the petitioner's motion has been submitted. Accordingly, petitioner's motion to quash the deposition subpoena is GRANTED. Counsel for the petitioner is directed to submit a proposed order to psw@hassard.com for review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)
